hypothesis: Introducing a bulky aromatic residue at position 5 and a hydrophobic residue at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 contacts, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design protocol generates candidates with strong ddG values but poor structural quality and docking scores. The primary bottleneck is structural validity, not sequence or stability. Tightening clash constraints and increasing candidate diversity will address this.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures (pLDDT=0.0) and poor docking scores suggest excessive clashes or poor geometry. Lowering the clash threshold will enforce better packing and geometry, improving structural quality and docking performance.

The low pass rate (12.5%) suggests the search space is not being adequately explored. Increasing the number of candidates will provide more diversity and increase the likelihood of generating a structurally valid and selective candidate.
